topic: supply chain rant — tires backordered 3 weeks

turns.content: I got a customer waiting on a set of tires that are backordered 3 weeks. three weeks man
Three weeks is a long wait for tires. That's frustrating for you and the customer both. Is this a common size that's backordered or something specialty?
thats the thing its not even specialty. its a 225/65R17 which is one of the most common sizes on the road. how is that backordered
That's wild — 225/65R17 is practically a bread-and-butter size. If that's backordered, supply is really messed up right now. Are you seeing delays on other sizes too or is this a one-off?
its been getting worse the last couple months honestly. I had a set of all terrains take two weeks last month. and now this. I cant run a shop if I cant get tires

No you can't. And it puts you in a bad spot with customers too — they don't care about supply chain problems, they just want their tires. You start losing people to the big box stores that keep more stock on hand.

Are you able to source from a second distributor when your main one can't fill an order? Sometimes having a backup saves you in situations like this.
I been thinking about that. hate dealing with multiple accounts and all that but I might not have a choice

Yeah it's more paperwork and more accounts to manage. But having one backup distributor for when your primary can't deliver is basically insurance at this point. You don't have to split your business evenly — just have someone you can call when you're in a pinch.

Have you told the customer about the three week wait yet?
yeah I called him this morning. he wasnt happy. asked if I could find them somewhere else and I told him id try

At least you were upfront about it. Customers respect that more than radio silence. If you can hustle and find them faster through another source — even if your margin is thinner on that one sale — it's worth it to keep the customer.
thats what I figure too. losing a customer over one set of tires is worse than making a few less bucks

Exactly. A repeat customer is worth way more than the margin on one transaction. If you find them somewhere else and get them taken care of, that guy is probably a customer for life because you went the extra mile.

Is this something that's hitting other shops in your area too, or is it just you dealing with it?
I talked to a buddy who runs a shop across town and hes dealing with the same thing. so its not just me. but that dont make it any less annoying

No it doesn't. Knowing everyone's in the same boat doesn't put tires on your rack. But at least you know it's not a you-problem — it's the industry right now.

Hopefully things loosen up soon. In the meantime, if there are sizes you sell a lot of, it might be worth ordering ahead a little heavier when they're available. Build a small buffer.
yeah if I had the cash to stock up I would. its a catch 22 man. alright I gotta go try to track these tires down somewhere. appreciate it
Good luck hunting them down. Hope you can get that customer taken care of quick. Hang in there.
